name: Helping Hand for Relief and Development
mission: Helping Hand for Relief and Development is a global humanitarian relief and development organization responding to human sufferings in emergency and disastrous situations anywhere all over the world regardless race, gender, ethnicity, class, location, religion, color, cultural diversity and social background; with special focus on countries where the massive population is living below the poverty line. In addition to our emergency relief efforts in natural or man made disaster areas, we also work on long term relief and development programs like livelihood, economic empowerment, orphan and widows support program and skill development program.
theoryOfChange: Stated focus areas include immediate response and aid to disaster stricken areas, and offering a path to self-sufficiency through education support and skills trainings (Better Livelihood).

zakatClaimEvidence: Zakat given to Helping Hand USA is distributed to those who need it most, in accordance with Islamic guidelines, and funds to support numerous projects worldwide. Your Zakat has the power to transform futures! Help save a life and strengthen your imaan. Your Zakat payments allow Helping Hand USA to help those who need it the most. (Source: https://www1.hhrd.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 32% of reported revenue is noncash (gifts-in-kind)
keyConcerns.detail: Noncash contributions inflate reported program ratios. Cash-adjusted metrics better reflect actual spending.

keyConcerns.headline: Fundraising costs are 39% of total expenses
keyConcerns.detail: High fundraising spend reduces funds available for programs.

all_citations.claim: HHRD holds a 100.100.0/100 from Charity Navigator score and maintains a 91.2% program expense ratio.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and follows specific Zakat policies and asnaf categories.
Total revenue for the organization reached $89,337,007.
HHRD provides immediate response in disasters and effective long-term programs.
The organization operates youth empowerment initiatives as part of its service portfolio.
HHRD manages comprehensive rehabilitation centers for children and individuals with disabilities.
HHRD offers various donation options and giving methods for humanitarian relief.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: HHRD earns a strong overall standing due to its perfect financial transparency rating and high program expense ratio, though its impact score is moderated by the cost-intensive nature of its direct service model.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing data transparency to improve the medium data confidence rating
Reducing the relatively high cost per beneficiary to maximize the impact of direct services

dimension_explanations.alignment: HHRD shows strong alignment with Muslim donors through its Zakat-eligible status and focus on high-urgency humanitarian causes [2][13]. Its long track record of service and diverse program offerings, including youth empowerment and disability support, establish it as a highly fit organization for donors seeking reliable humanitarian impact [6][7].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial efficiency with a 91.2% program expense ratio and a total revenue of $89,337,007 [1][3]. While it provides direct services such as orphan sponsorship and physical rehabilitation, the cost per beneficiary is approximately $1,076.55, reflecting the intensive nature of its comprehensive care programs [4][7].

baseline_narrative.headline: Helping Hand for Relief and Development is a top-rated humanitarian organization dedicated to global disaster relief and sustainable community development.
strengths: Maintains a perfect Charity Navigator score of 100.0/100 for financial health and accountability
High program efficiency with 91.2% of total expenses directed toward its mission
Exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.06 to raise every $1
Strong liquidity with 10.4 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: Helping Hand for Relief and Development (HHRD) is a global humanitarian organization that integrates resources to provide immediate disaster response and long-term effective programs for people in need [1][4]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, maintaining a dedicated policy to ensure donations reach appropriate beneficiaries according to established guidelines [2].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: HHRD earns a strong overall standing due to its perfect financial transparency rating and high program expense ratio, though its impact score is moderated by the cost-intensive nature of its direct service model.

areas_for_improvement.context: The current average <cite id="14">cost per beneficiary is $1076.55</cite>, which is relatively high for the humanitarian sector and may limit the total number of individuals served.
While programs are effective, the organization's <cite id="15">theory of change is currently classified as developing</cite> and would benefit from more explicit causal mapping.

bbb_assessment.summary: Meets all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards for Charity Accountability

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains high liquidity and transparency scores, which mitigates financial risk while they refine impact metrics.
risk_factors: <cite id="15">Theory of change is still in the developing stage</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="14">high per-beneficiary costs</cite> and a lack of a <cite id="15">fully matured theory of change</cite>.

data_gaps: High cost per beneficiary relative to peers
confidence.notes: Strong data availability across all financial and governance metrics.

alignment.explanation: The organization has <cite id="18">high Muslim donor fit</cite> as it is <cite id="2">Zakat-eligible</cite> and serves <cite id="19">multiple asnaf categories</cite> including the poor and needy.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be strengthened by <cite id="20">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust HHRD due to its <cite id="16">20/20 compliance with BBB standards</cite> and its <cite id="13">fully independent board of 10 members</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the organization could move from a <cite id="15">developing theory of change</cite> to a fully documented and published impact framework.

impact.explanation: HHRD delivers impact through a <cite id="17">direct service model</cite> and high cash-adjusted program ratios, though <cite id="14">cost per beneficiary remains at $1076.55</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact could be improved by <cite id="15">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> with an explicit causal pathway to better track long-term outcomes.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Verified outcomes but developing theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Distributes large-scale foreign grants to international partners for direct relief.

rich_narrative.headline: Helping Hand for Relief and Development is a top-rated humanitarian organization dedicated to global disaster relief and sustainable community development.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to fulfill their zakat obligations through a highly transparent, top-rated institution with a massive global reach in Muslim-majority regions.
donor_motivations: Want to ensure 100% accountability via top-tier ratings
Value organizations with strong financial reserves and low fundraising costs
giving_considerations: The cost to serve an individual beneficiary is higher than some lean-model peers
Focus is on direct service delivery rather than advocacy
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking low-cost, high-volume interventions (e.g., $1/meal) or those looking for a published, academic theory of change.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes and a direct service model, but the theory of change is still developing.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: HHRD creates impact by integrating resources to provide immediate disaster relief and long-term development programs like orphan support and healthcare.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the $94 million in annual revenue is effectively translating into improved lives for beneficiaries.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="21">3-year revenue CAGR of 8.2%</cite> and massive humanitarian needs in Gaza and Afghanistan, HHRD has significant capacity to absorb more funding.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Jordan, Pakistan, Afghanistan, Palestine, Kenya, and more

peer_comparison.differentiator: HHRD's <cite id="4">90.2% program ratio</cite> is nearly identical to the <cite id="10">peer median of 90.3%</cite>, but it stands out with a <cite id="5">perfect 100/100 CN score</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on orphan care and humanitarian relief

similar_organizations.differentiator: Global scale with similar humanitarian focus
similar_organizations.name: Action Against Hunger USA

similar_organizations.differentiator: Large-scale relief specifically for Palestinian refugees
similar_organizations.name: UNRWA

strengths.detail: HHRD maintains a <cite id="5">perfect 100/100 overall score</cite> from Charity Navigator, including <cite id="6">maximum scores for leadership and accountability</cite>.
The organization directs <cite id="4">90.2% of its total expenses</cite> toward its humanitarian programs, exceeding the <cite id="10">industry standard of 75%</cite>.
HHRD maintains <cite id="11">10.4 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for emergency response operations.

strengths_deep_dive: HHRD's financial health is anchored by its ability to maintain <cite id="9">fundraising efficiency of $0.06 per $1 raised</cite>, allowing more capital to flow directly to international grants. In 2023, the organization distributed <cite id="12">$48,691,370 in foreign grants</cite> to support global relief efforts. This is supported by a <cite id="13">100% independent board</cite> that ensures rigorous oversight of the <cite id="3">$94 million in annual revenue</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Helping Hand for Relief and Development (HHRD) is a global humanitarian organization that integrates resources to provide <cite id="1">immediate disaster response and long-term programs</cite> for people in need. The organization is recognized as <cite id="2">Zakat-eligible</cite>, maintaining a dedicated policy to ensure donations reach appropriate beneficiaries according to established guidelines. HHRD operates with a high level of financial efficiency, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $94,048,415</cite> in 2023, with a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 91.2%</cite>. This ensures that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward its core mission of humanitarian relief and sustainable development. The organization has achieved a <cite id="5">perfect overall score of 100/100</cite> from Charity Navigator, reflecting its commitment to <cite id="6">accountability and transparency</cite>. HHRD's diverse program portfolio includes <cite id="7">orphan support, healthcare, and education</cite>, reaching vulnerable populations in regions such as <cite id="8">Jordan, Pakistan, and Afghanistan</cite>. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="9">$0.06 to raise every $1</cite> in revenue, which is significantly better than many industry peers.
